_comment: Kalos (XY) Overheard lines. Best-effort gen-6 flavor; verified:false until QA'd (CURATION_TODO.md). Gen 6 is 3D — no overworld trainer sprites, so lines are text-only with real class names.

vaniville-town.text: Your new neighbors just moved in! The Professor already sent word about you kids.

aquacorde-town.text: Everyone meets at the café tables by the river. That's where journeys start around here!

santalune-city.text: The Gym here is inside a giant spider's web — the Leader photographs Bug Pokémon!

lumiose-city.text: The City of Light! Two hundred streets, five Pokémon Centers, and the Prism Tower Gym!

camphrier-town.text: An old, old town under an old, old castle. The Shabboneau family fell on hard times…

cyllage-city.text: Beach on one side, cliffs for climbing on the other. Our Gym is a rock wall you scale!

ambrette-town.text: The Fossil Lab is here! They can bring Pokémon back from ancient stones.

geosenge-town.text: The strange stones standing around town? Nobody knows who placed them. Or why.

shalour-city.text: The Tower of Mastery rises from the bay — the secret of Mega Evolution sleeps inside.

coumarine-city.text: Half port, half hillside — ride the monorail between them! The Gym's up in a great tree.

laverre-city.text: Our Gym is inside a dollhouse of a tree, and our Leader is a famous designer!

dendemille-town.text: A little farming town between the mountains. The windmill keeps turning through the snow.

anistar-city.text: The great pink sundial by the sea… they say it came from space, and glows at dusk.

couriway-town.text: Waterfalls on every side! The train from the far region used to stop at this station.

snowbelle-city.text: It never stops snowing here. The Gym is a palace of ice — dress warm!

kiloude-city.text: You can only reach this southern city by TMV! The Battle Maison never closes.

santalune-forest.text: This forest feels just like one in a faraway region… Bug Pokémon love it here.

parfum-palace.text: Admission is 1,000 Poké! The chandeliers alone are worth a fortune, mon ami.

connecting-cave.text: They call it Zubat Cave for a reason. Bring earplugs!

glittering-cave.text: The crystals down here glow on their own — and fossils sleep in the walls.

reflection-cave.text: The mirrored walls show a dozen of you. Some say the reflections aren't all yours…

frost-cavern.text: The deeper you climb, the colder it gets. An abominable Pokémon guards the heart of it.

terminus-cave.text: Miners abandoned these tunnels. They say a legendary serpent coils in the deepest pit.

lost-hotel.text: A grand hotel, ruined and dark. The punks who squat there won't say what happened.

kalos-power-plant.text: The badlands plant powers Lumiose's tower. Strange folk in red suits were sniffing around…

poke-ball-factory.text: Every Poké Ball in Kalos rolls off this line! …Why is the conveyor running backward?

azure-bay.text: Islands, sea grass, and the old Sea Spirit's Den out in the middle. Great Surfing!

sea-spirits-den.text: A tiny cave in the bay where the sea spirit is said to rest. Fishermen leave it offerings.

chamber-of-emptiness.text: A bare stone chamber with nothing inside. Why does it feel like something's watching?

pokemon-village.text: Deep past the forest, mistreated Pokémon live free. A very wise one watches over them.

kalos-victory-road.text: The final gauntlet — ruins, caves, and cliffs stacked between you and the League.

kalos-pokemon-league.text: A castle of four chambers — Fire, Water, Steel, Dragon — and the Champion's hall above.

kalos-route-1.text: The shortest route in the world! Just a garden path between two towns, really.

kalos-route-2.text: Your first tall grass! The Professor's friends will show you how catching works.

kalos-route-3.text: Ponds, songbirds, and a gentle road into the forest. Perfect first stroll.

kalos-route-4.text: A parterre garden all the way to Lumiose — hedges, roses, and fountain mazes!

kalos-route-5.text: Roller skaters own this road! Grind the rails down toward the old town.

kalos-route-6.text: The tree-lined drive to Parfum Palace. Fancy folk, fancier Pokémon.

kalos-route-7.text: The riverside road past the Day Care — and a very large Pokémon asleep on the bridge!

kalos-route-8.text: The cliff road forks: down to the fossil beach, or along the bluffs to the sea.

kalos-route-9.text: Too rocky to walk — you cross the badlands on a rented Rhyhorn!

kalos-route-10.text: Rows of strange standing stones line this road… like a field of monuments.

kalos-route-11.text: Crystals bloom right out of the hillside here, all the way to the mirrored cave.

kalos-route-12.text: The seaside ranch road! Skiddo graze free — you can even ride them.

kalos-route-13.text: Wind turbines tower over the badlands. The power plant hides in the dunes.

kalos-route-14.text: A swamp of dead trees and deep bog. The scarecrows… some of them move.

kalos-route-15.text: Autumn leaves and fallen logs — the ruined hotel squats at the road's end.

kalos-route-16.text: The mushroom marsh road! Locals harvest them by the basket.

kalos-route-17.text: Snow too deep to walk — a Mamoswine carries you across the drifts!

kalos-route-18.text: The old mining valley. Rails and carts everywhere, and the cave mouth gaping above.

kalos-route-19.text: The great bridge over the marsh — you can see half of southern Kalos from the middle.

kalos-route-20.text: The winding woods play tricks — paths that loop, clearings that move.

kalos-route-21.text: The last stretch before Victory Road, half river, half ruin.

kalos-route-22.text: Détourner Way loops behind the badlands — a shortcut for those who know it.
